original_image_tags: A single page of aged, light brown or beige paper, oriented vertically. The paper shows signs of wear, including a tattered and torn left edge. The content consists almost entirely of very faint, illegible text that appears to be a "bleed-through" or ghost image from the reverse side of the page. Two distinct stamps are visible.

elements.description: Reverse side of a document page with several stamps and faint bleed-through text. Top right: inverted blue 'RECEIVED' stamp, dated 'FEB 28 1950' and 'OP.32315'. Top left: circular 'BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION' seal stamp with 'MAR 1 6 1950'. Center left: red handwritten number '518'. Faint, largely illegible typed text is visible as bleed-through from the opposite side.

judge.reason: Description B reads specific, structured text from stamps (e.g., 'BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION', 'MAR 1 6 1950', '518') while Description A explicitly calls the content largely illegible, creating a direct contradiction on the legibility and specific quoted text of the visual content.
